title: Level 818

|body=
I mean, you never expect it to happen in your community, right? It's only something that could happen on TV, your family is protected by God and all. People get unlucky all the time and you try to come to terms with that before it comes for you. But you can never be prepared. That's not how life works. Life doesn't wait for you to be ready, does it?
You can stop if you'd like...

No, no. I'm not done. Just... give me a moment. Okay, I'm here. God doesn't wait for you to be ready to fight his battles, he puts you in these trials to show his glory. I don't know where I'm trying to go with that... but yeah. I wasn't prepared for it. No one else knew he'd do that, and now my daughter is gone.
If you could say anything to █████████████, what would you tell him?

Please, don't hurt her. Find it in your heart to keep her safe, even if it means you get to keep her. She's my baby... and I don't want anything bad to happen to her. But even if that means never seeing her again, I hope that you can find the strength to keep her safe. Maybe one day, even bring her back. As long as you take care of her, I won't press charges. I promise.
[[File:801Photo.png|center|thumb|600px|
Level_818-01.png
]]
LEVEL '''818'''
THREAT INDEX
Class 4

'''Exit:''' 5/5
Rarely Occuring Exits
'''Environment:''' 2/5
Decent Environmental Risk
'''Entities:''' 4/5
Significant Entity Population

Description

+++Level 818 is a collection of non-anomalous parks and playgrounds randomly laid out across a seemingly infinite plane. +++The playgrounds found in Level 818 are made out of ABS Plastic, and the ground around it is made up of foam. This foam flooring is arranged in a brick pattern, and is painted red. Unlike regular flooring, the foam has a springy property to it that allows it to easily cushion falls. Removing the flooring has shown it to be decently larger in size than its appearance in the ground, pointing towards the foam bricks to be packed together via physical tension. The bouncy quality of the floor has been attributed to various injuries sustained by those traveling through the level. Minor sprains and aches are to be expected from inexperienced wanderers. +++Each discovered play-set in Level 818 is completely unique. These sets come in 2 noteworthy sizes: large and small. Large play-sets take up roughly 700 square meters in size and are consistently more dense in play equipment in comparison to the smaller parks, which are about 450 square meters. Some playgrounds have an accompanying section, either a soccer field or baseball field. These sections match up perfectly to child-friendly stadiums of the same variety. All segments of the playground are mostly indestructible, with most common means of destruction ineffective against them. +++The park sections of Level 818 stretch across the level as a whole, rarely broken up by patches of forestry or playgrounds. The pathways are all made up of concrete, with bolted down benches and trash cans roughly 10 meters apart. These amenities are also made of ABS plastic, with even the nails being used to drill them in place made of the same compound. Some who have traveled through Level 818 have claimed to hear birds and the sounds of other people, despite the level being under the effect of the [[Phenomenon 15|Alone Phenomenon]]. The rest of the ground is made out of fake grass, physically similar to football turf. The turf shares the same springy phenomenon as the foam terra, though it is less bouncy as the latter. Despite the dirt being fake, trees, alongside other shrubbery, regularly pepper the landscape. Outside of entities, these are the only living beings in Level 818.

Entities

+++According to various reports, there is a significant bug population within Level 818, noticeably different than other levels. These insects include normal bugs, and other insect entities native to the Backrooms—such as [[Cone-Spiders]] and [[Entity 34|Blood Wasps]]. All bugs found in Level 818 are omnivorous, with them consuming tree products and other entities to survive. The full food chain of these entities is unclear, due to the dangers of traveling through the level. Other bugs found in Level 818—such as [[Entity 124|Clockroaches]]—are known to consume corpses, the origin of the cadavers remains unknown. +++Large patches of [[Entity 53|False Grass]] have also been reported in Level 818, often being positioned in the middle of park fields. False Grass has been mainly seen trying to trap other entities found in Level 818, such as [[Entity 104|Sentinels]]. How False Grass consumes and hunts does not show any change. However, because Level 818 is made up of turf, False Grass often appears more discolored than in other levels. The entity often takes a dry yellow appearance, making it easier for observant wanderers to differentiate it from the rest of the terra in Level 818. This is presumed to be due to the lack of soil making it harder for False Grass to retain water.

Communities

While Level 818 looks to be suitable for life, the [[Phenomenon 15|Alone Phenomenon]] and other dangers present make the level unfit for habitation.

Entrances & Exits

+++Because of the large size of Level 818, finding proper exits is significantly difficult. Often, wanderers are only able to escape through random [[No-Clipping|noclipping]], which may be reliable due to the unnatural stability of Level 818. The entrances and exits compiled are up to date as of '''March 14th, 2022'''.

Entrances

* Breaking through rarely appearing windows in [[Level 287]] will cause wanderers to noclip and enter Level 818. Some windows found have been disguised [[Entity 2|predatory windows]]. * Traveling deep enough through Sector 2 of [[The Core]] may provide passage to Level 818 through a yellow slide. * Finding space turf in [[Level 15]] and clipping into it will bring them to Level 818. * Drowning in gray pools within [[Level 165]] may allow entry into Level 818, though other levels are connected to this point as well. * Long strips of road in [[Level 667]] may transform into Level 818. If the sun appears to rise while traveling, this indicates a successful transformation.

Exits

* Finding puddles in Level 818 then jumping in them may give access to [[Level 413]]. This entry often requires significant and repeated attempts. * Bright red bricks in Level 818, when dug up, will open up into [[Level 140]]. * The small forests in Level 818 may have holes hidden in the middle, traveling into these will cause the wander to clip and enter [[Level 990]]. * Through extremely rare spacial phenomenon, stepping inside beams of light in Level 818 will send wanderers into [[Level 401]]. * Pitfalls across Level 818, if entered, may bring people to [[Level 722]]. * Rarely appearing in skate parks are specialized lavatory buildings. Spending 20 minutes or more will have people clip into [[Level 31]].
{{Fullscreen|number=1|padding=2rem|color=black |hide=Close Narrative |show=
Open Narrative
|content=
Brushing past the remaining few cars, the silent hum of a beat up Camaro filled the sky in what would otherwise be a quiet evening. The late sermon at the church came and went with little fanfare. Penny could almost hear the pastor droning on in her mind as the afternoon service went on. She didn't bother to ask what he was preaching, the man bored her enough. It was why she moved her sermons to the morning, allowing an easy exit out before he took the stage. Call it sinful, Penny never cared, the wondrous tendrils of God loosened their grip on her day after day. Slowly, the car would come to a stop, poorly parked in one of the few dozen left about the front of the church. No one else was going to be here, at least that she knew of. As she stepped out, her eyes caught onto a shifting vehicle closing in on her. But as she turned around, there was nothing to greet her. Just the soft course of the wind and cars only a few meters away. The church keys met a familiar lock, clicking and whirring as they allowed entry into the chapel. Penny stepped inside, with the door closing in a familiar clunk, which softly echoed off the walls. Before her laid the main church hallway, which grew more threatening as the sky darkened. To her right, was a dirty wooden pedestal, still caked in the dirt from events only days ago. A collection of mints sat there, inviting her to take one. But as she looked towards them, perhaps to give some sort of security to the mess, thoughts of her daughter flashed before her. The childish greed of collecting more mints than was necessary, turning a breath aid into a cool candy. Penny's arm, which was once outstretched to grab one of them, now pulled away, wincing as her mind reminded her of what was lost. It was a deep ache that her prayers didn't seem good enough to heal. That's why Penny came back to the church. Her spirits were crushed by a man she thought she knew. One step after another, Penny slowly walked down the chapel hallway. She didn't bother to turn on any lights, looking into the darkness with steel eyes and soul. She could see the silhouette of the man who betrayed her. Past the gymnasium entrance she stepped, her thoughts clouded her mind again like an influence of the devil. At least, Penny thought it was the devil. She spent so long following the footsteps of God that each error she was quick to pin on the snake. Yet it grew harder every time, especially with her girl gone. She could sense the influence of the deceiver in the man who took her away, tingling on her wrinkled skin like that of Spiderman. Penny did everything she should have to set her daughter on the right path. But one bad apple ruins the orchard. At least, that's what Penny remembered hearing. Reaching the entrance of the church, she looked towards the sound both first, the small stair inclined that came towards it. That very morning she could recall speaking to one of the men who ran it. Shaun... wasn't it? She couldn't recall the conversation she had with him, only a few moments after where she joked to the man's son about him and Amy getting together. Shaun laughed, the boy didn't, only rolling her eyes at the possibility. "Christians need to stick together." She always said, especially in that moment as she joked with the boy. "Amy could use some better influence once she comes back." It was met with a soft laugh from the other man, and only a sigh from his son. Penny didn't much like the boy either, thought he was easy to influence too. Hell, it was the topic of her sermon for the past few weeks. She had seen with many how easily some had turned to a life of sin. Penny knew many of their sins tenderly, being also one of the most active members of their community. She knew they needed that reality check, and that's what her lessons were all about. Fighting not only the failures of other fallen men, but those of your own. Yet, no matter what lessons she tried on Amy, nothing worked. And now. she was gone. Amy had too become lost like those on the streets. Penny stepped into the chapel to solve that, looking towards the iron-built crucifix as she kneeled at the altar. The air was tense with a sense of power, one that only felt like the majesty of a god working through her. Not just any god, her god. Her hands clasped softly around each other, and Penny dug her elbows into the cushion covering. Quietly, she started to mutter to herself. It was barely audible over the other noises the church had. Even in silence, she was the smallest voice. There wasn't a need for volume when speaking to God, just earnestly. Penny believed she was being earnest enough. It had to be. ''Click'' The sound of the door opening behind her made Penny jump to her feet, her breath got caught in her throat, creating a still web of shock. Entering the room was the church security guard, Maverick. Quickly, she lost her panic, and even smiled a little. Penny had found quite the liking to Maverick, often reminding him he was her second favorite person after Jesus. Though when Maverick looked back at her, he had discomfort on his face. This was one of the older women who kept bothering him after service. So many of them just saw themselves above the baseline rules, and would shoot their shots with a taken man. And yet, each time Maverick reminded them, it went in one ear and out the other. "Hello Maverick," she spoke softly, Penny's shoulders visibly relaxing as she looked towards someone she liked. He responded with a small nod, leaving the chapel in silence for a while before he coughed and spoke up. "You're not supposed to be here, you know that right?" Maverick asked, tucking his arms against his chest as he spoke. He certainly wasn't a bodybuilder type, but his youth and adherence to physical fitness made him visibly stronger across all the fronts. Suppose Penny liked that about him, his strength. And he seemed to be enough of a good Christian too, with his active work in the community. Penny had tried to talk him into spending time together elsewhere, yet he always seemed to decline. What was she lacking? Sure she was old and wrinkly, with a wide lower body and spindly limbs, but that was just on the exterior. How sinful would that be just to judge the exterior of someone? "Then why are you here?" Penny responded, the southern drawl in her voice on full display. "Same reason I always am," he huffed, walking closer to Penny before passing by her. "Because someone entered the church and now I need to figure out who did it." Maverick punctuated the thought with a sigh, looking over Penny lazily. His mouth was flat, slightly down-turned; there was more on his mind than just getting Penny out of the building. It was a sixth sense the older women had. "God doesn't like it when you lie." "Graah! Fine! I'm here to pray for my wife." With a grunt, he got onto his knees, turning away from Penny and towards the back of the chapel. Its grand array of rows were created with dark wood and red cushioning. Neither of them knew where the wood came from, but it was reliable, so no one really was worried. Attached to the seats were slots for bibles, leather bound packets of knowledge and guidance. Penny looked at Maverick disapprovingly, ticking in a sorrowful way before speaking again. "You're supposed to look toward the crucifix when you pray. You can't turn your back on God." He shot a glare at Penny, leaving her with a sour taste in her mouth as he shifted around. Yet when she watched him start to pray again, speaking in a low tone, it filled her with a feeling she could not articulate, but had to act upon. Though slow steps, she moved closer to Maverick, watching his sealed eyes and mutterings. Slowly, her hand reached out to touch Maverick's hair. For a lapse, she thought he wouldn't notice, but he jerked his head away with a fury. Maverick got up with a grunt, looking down at the middle aged woman angrily. "Stop fucking touching me." "Language." "I don't care. Every time I try to do my job, you or one of your friends comes and harasses me. Every time I look for peace, you mess it up by being weird. I'm so damn tired of your advances Penny. I'm also sick of Brenda, Karen, and Lucy too. You're lucky the church doesn't listen to my complaints, you should've been out of her a long ass time ago." He snapped at Penny, leaving the chapel with the heavy thuds of his shoes reverberating across the space. Penny was left still, trying to process his mad ramblings, before her eyes went to the cross. "Please Lord, bring him back to you. I see him trying to run away..." she murmured, making a cross gesture with her hands before turning her back on the grand statue. Slowly, she moved out of the church, her thoughts about her daughter were replaced with Maverick. What did she do to deserve that response? All she did was touch her hair. Same with the others, they haven't done anything mean to him. It seemed so illogical. So not Christian...
[[File:AbandonedCars.png|center|500px|frameless]]
“Thanks for taking me back home,” Amy contently sighed, with the soft jazzy tunes on the radio slowly fading away as it was silenced. The air tasted of fresh rainfall, and the glistening of the trees under the moonlight filled what would be a dimly lit road with character. The stars above slowly shifted through the night sky, omnipresent and unchanging. It was the same sky Amy looked at when she was a little girl, and the same one she shared a playful kiss under. Nothing had changed about the stars, but a lot had changed for her. Perhaps it was that stagnation and reliability that she’d come to respect. It did not bow to the will of others, instead having others bow to it. But as she stopped reflecting on the cosmos, her pale brown eyes looked to her parents home, no different from the ones around it. She could only remember it because of the number on the front. 818 Keystone Road. Her contentment was soured by bitterness as she braced stepping back inside. If Amy was lucky, she could tiptoe past her parents bedroom and hear their tirades in the morning. If not… she didn’t want to think about it for too long. They never cared if she was safe, only if she was with certain people and at certain places. Neither of those things were true for Amy. It left a dirty taste on her lips to see them again, but it had to be this way. There was no way out of the suburbs she was raised in, certainly not one that fit her budget. It was the small paying day jobs that barely had enough money to repair the leaking sink, again, or it was the internship that made you work for nothing for the chance that you could get a better pay later on. It was comparing apples to oranges, except both were rotten. “You don’t look good, sis.” The driver muttered, it was a close friend of Amy’s, but one that was never approved of. She was punkish and witty, disrespectful and brash, and continually acted against the better wishes of the town. But with no one else to push against them, many of the town’s rejects gravitated toward her. Amy was one of the few where that feeling was reciprocated. “Should we take a couple laps around so you can tell me what’s up?” “That… would be nice actually.” Amy turned to face the other girl, with the hum of the engine bouncing off the vehicle walls, an imperfect melody. Lora slowly pulled the car out of its parking spot, slowly traveling across the barren stretches of road. Each crunch of the unpaved asphalt seemed to extend outward infinitely, with nothing around to bounce the sound back to the duo. Before the vehicle was a strip of empty land, only punctuated by various houses and the occasional turn. Lora’s attention slowly dragged itself back to the passenger, as they made their first turn. “Go on, spill.” “Right, well,” Amy stumbled through her words, trying to assemble an explanation with the mess of distorted thoughts. It snagged like a fish to a rod, leaving her to groan out of frustration. Lora laughed some, but it didn’t feel tasteless. Nothing felt disrespectful about the way Lora acted and spoke, it was just, different. She carried herself differently. The words she spoke were different too, but they still had value to them. Amy didn’t quite know whether it was wrong or not, as the hard lines of good and evil she was once taught had begun to slip over time. It became undefinable and amorphous, replacing what was supposed to be true with questions, not answers. What was the point of change when the change was already planned? Why do those who deserve death get infinite life? Is it the duty of one above the law to still follow them? The questions stuck to Amy well, welded into her mind. She managed to push them away again, taking a deep breath before looking towards the road ahead, just as Lora took another right turn. “You know my mom pretty well at this point, so I won’t dabble into all that shit. Well, she’s gotten into these weird practices of… claiming I'm gone? I don’t really know how to explain it, but ever since I started hanging out with you more, she would claim that I was lost and that someone needed to bring me back. She refuses to accept me for me, and has chosen to live a life of pedantry and intolerance for the only child left in the household.” Amy sighed again, a heavy weight on her chest. Her head would tilt downward looking at her legs and arms. “It’s been getting harder to accept that within myself. Sometimes I look at myself in the mirror and I wonder ‘‘where did that good girl go?’’ It sometimes feels like I killed the person she wanted me to be, and now she's hoping that the corpse will magically come back to life. And I know that I am me and all that’s happened is true and valid—but—sometimes it’s hard to remind myself of that.” Amy whispered out her final thoughts, pressing her hands into her face and sealing her eyes shut. In her pained silence, she prayed. Amy didn’t know who she was praying to, but it was a desperate plea to whatever god heard her cries.
''Please make me whole again''
''Please don’t leave me like this''
''Please take me back into your arms''
''Please''
''Please make me normal''
''Or take me somewhere that will care''
Lora thought for a while, leaving a warm-hearted silence as the car continued to move. It was a red camry, with so many scratches embedded into the paint it seemed like a tiger had previously driven it. Despite its unsavory appearance, and the weird sounds the brake made, it was reliable, and that was more than enough for Lora and Amy. The seats had their share of use, with the drivers side never able to get rid of a grungy scent, nor stitch the leather cover back to its clothen underbelly. Amy had also learned to tolerate it, there weren't many others who could reliably drive her around. Amy’s parents never wished to get her a car, and the money she had wasn’t enough for the prices in the area either. It was a brick wall of progress she simply could not pass through, lest she be crushed between the twisted games of life. “I wish I could give you the assurance you’re looking for,” Lora started, parking the car again. Unlike earlier, they now were a considerable distance away from Amy’s home, yet still surrounded in the suburban sprawl. The trees crouched over them, dappling soft moonlight onto the car’s roof. Before Lora spoke again, she turned on the vehicle’s only light, covering them in a dim yellow glow. Even with such little to provide, the light did so proudly, casting a soft warmth on Lora and Amy. “Really, I would, but I’m no wizard. Really the only way you can be happy with your identity is if you’re certain of it internally. From there, it doesn’t matter what the others do, because you know you better than them.” “What does that have to do with the disappearance shit?” “I’m getting there. There comes a point in knowing yourself where it doesn’t matter how others respond. Yes it’s validating for someone to call you the way you want to be, but basing your identity on other people’s responses is never going to end well.” Amy sighed, nodding along as Lora spoke. In a cruel timing, as Lora finished talking, the groans of another vehicle passed by them. It was an old, tan, Camaro. As it drove past them Amy winced, her body shriveling up as she remembered what her mother asked of her. “Is something wrong?” Lora asked. Amy nodded. “Was that your mom?” Amy nodded. “Were you supposed to be home by now?” Amy nodded. “Shit.” Lora muttered, looking as the tan car turned onto one of the other streets. Was there any other choice to make? Lora couldn’t house Amy, nor did she know anyone who could. “I’m sorry… I have to bring you back… we’ll talk in the morning ok?” Amy nodded. Amy went home. Minutes, hours, and days passed. Lora checked her phone, hoping to whatever god that existed that Amy would one day send a message to her. Or she’d appear at their local college again. Anything to prove that Amy was still around. But she never showed up. Penny still complained about losing her daughter, and Lora watched her lamentations with ire. She knew the truth.
